name: Rough.js (Hand-Drawn Style Graphics)

performance.notes: Lightweight; hand-drawn effect computation is fast

agent_readiness.notes: Hand-drawn sketch-style graphics. Canvas: const rc = rough.canvas(document.getElementById('canvas')). rc.rectangle(10, 10, 200, 200, {roughness: 2, fill: 'red'}). rc.circle(100, 100, 50, {fillStyle: 'hachure'}). rc.line(0, 0, 200, 200). rc.polygon([[0,0],[200,0],[200,200]]). rc.path('M 0 0 L 100 100'). SVG: const svg = rough.svg(svgElement). svg.rectangle(...). Options: roughness (0=smooth, 10=very rough), bowing, seed, fill, fillStyle (hachure/dots/cross-hatch/solid), stroke, strokeWidth, hachureAngle. Excalidraw uses Rough.js for its drawing style. React: react-rough-fiber.

gotchas: Hand-drawn effect is non-deterministic by default — use seed option for reproducible output
Not a full graphics library — only basic shapes (rectangle, circle, line, polygon, path)
fillStyle='hachure' is the famous sketchy fill but has many tuneable params
SVG output may differ from Canvas output in edge cases
No text rendering — combine with native Canvas text API
best_when: Wireframes, diagrams, and presentations needing hand-drawn aesthetic
Educational content wanting approachable visual style
avoid_when: Precise technical diagrams where hand-drawn style is inappropriate
Complex graphics with many elements — overhead of roughness computation adds up
When deterministic output is critical without seed management
summary: Rough.js creates hand-drawn style graphics. rough.canvas(el).rectangle(x,y,w,h, {roughness:2, fill:'red', fillStyle:'hachure'}). Canvas and SVG backends. MIT by Preet Shihn. Type-annotated. Used by Excalidraw. Best for sketch-style wireframes and informal visualizations. Use seed for reproducible output. Basic shapes only (rect, circle, line, path). No text rendering. Non-deterministic by default.
